name: Minor Qi Pill
description: A cloudy pill used by outer disciples to gather a small amount of qi.

name: Outer Disciple Token
description: A wooden token proving provisional acceptance into the Azure Cloud Sect.

name: Mist Wolf Core
description: A faint spirit core taken from a mist wolf. Useful for pills, formations, or sect contribution.

name: Crude Qi Elixir
description: A rough village elixir. It helps stir qi, but its impurities make elders frown.

name: Bamboo Staff
description: A plain bamboo pole cut for reach. It will not impress a cultivator, but it keeps desperate hands farther away.

name: Reinforced Walking Staff
description: A plain staff strengthened with iron bands at the village forge. It is not an immortal weapon, but the mountain road is still a road.

name: Family Sewing Roll
description: Plain cloth, bone needles, waxed thread, and folded patches given by family before the road could become dangerous.

name: Aunt Lin's Herb Papers
description: Small folded papers marked with Aunt Lin's notes for wrapping bitter roots, wound powder, and roadside poultices.

name: Guo's Wire And Rivets
description: A palm-sized twist of wire, rivets, and useful scrap from Blacksmith Guo's forge. Nothing glamorous, which is why it works.

name: Old Ren's Road Knife
description: A short utility knife from Old Ren, dull enough to be humble and sharp enough to cut cord, bark, bandage, or trouble.

name: Mortal Repair Bundle
description: A complete field bundle assembled from village gifts: cloth, herb papers, wire, rivets, and a road knife. Good enough for crude travel repairs and simple roadside preparation.

name: Child's Cloud Jade
description: A small jade pendant carried by the immortal child you rescued from the bandit road. Its cloud mark feels warmer than plain stone.

name: Roadside Whisper Notes
description: Scraps of merchant gossip about talismans, sect debts, and a black-thread token seen near the broken trade road.

name: Cloud-Iron Field Manual
description: A stained repair manual for keeping carts, weapons, and simple spirit-worked fittings alive under road pressure.

name: Silk Lotus Hairpin
description: A pale hairpin left by the injured immortal girl after you treated her wounds. A faint medicinal fragrance clings to it.

name: Azure Cloud Trial Token
description: A black-threaded bronze token marked with a cloud-over-mountain symbol. It hums faintly when turned toward the north.

name: Frost-Vein Herb
description: A pale spirit herb that grows near cold streams in the lower grove. Medicine disciples use it for simple qi-soothing brews.

name: Cloud Moss
description: Soft silver-green moss that gathers around damp stones touched by thin mountain qi.

name: Low-Grade Beast Bone
description: A small spirit beast bone left in the brush. It can be traded, studied, or used in crude crafting later.

name: Cooling Herb Salve
description: A clean herbal salve for cuts, burns, and road wounds. It restores a modest amount of health.

name: Simple Field Bandages
description: Clean strips, moss padding, and a little bitter paste. Not elegant medicine, but enough to stop bleeding on the road.

name: Bone-Reinforced Staff
description: A staff strengthened with low-grade beast bone. Still crude, but much more dangerous in trained hands.

name: Mist-Core Qi Draught
description: A qi draught refined from a mist wolf core and ice-vein herb. The icy trace sharpens the flood of qi into a pressure that can crack a mortal stage ceiling.

name: Ice-Vein Herb
description: A stiff-stemmed herb that grows on cold, permanently shaded stone faces. Its sap carries a faint icy qi trace used in breakthrough elixirs.

name: Beast Hide
description: Thick hide stripped from a spirit boar. The qi residue in the bristles makes it tougher than ordinary leather, useful for crude armour or trade.

name: Road Copper
description: A handful of worn copper coins taken from bandits — too few to matter to a sect disciple, too many for a hungry man to leave behind.

name: Shadow Viper Gland
description: The venom gland of a shadow viper, carefully extracted whole. Medicine disciples use it in anti-venom preparations. It smells faintly of turned earth.

name: Viper Scale
description: A dozen finger-nail-sized scales from a shadow viper's flank. The colour shifts between brown and black depending on the angle of light.

name: Cloud Stag Antler
description: A pale antler shed from a cloud stag, still faintly charged with the creature's qi. Prized in medicine and formation work alike. Elders pay above standard rate for clean specimens.

name: Spirit Beast Blood
description: Collected blood from a spirit beast, sealed in a small clay vial. The qi in it makes it useful for alchemical preparations, body tempering compounds, and certain formation inks.

name: Stone Crab Shell
description: A fragment of shell from a stone crab, roughly the size of a palm. The outer face has a natural hardness that resists most mortal-grade cutting edges.

name: Crab Core
description: A dense qi core taken from the body cavity of a stone crab. Its qi is earthy and stable — less potent than a wolf core, but more consistent.

name: Iron Shortsword
description: A plain road-forged shortsword, single-edged and notched from use. It is heavier than it looks and adequately balanced for someone who trained without a master.

name: Tattered Sect Notes
description: Torn pages from what was once an outer disciple's cultivation journal, overwritten with strange annotations in a different hand. Some passages reference a formation beneath the broken trade road.

name: Shade Essence
description: A cold condensate left behind after a mist shade dissipates. It is not quite solid. Medicine elders classify it as a rare-tier ingredient for certain qi-disruption resistance compounds.

name: Mist Residue
description: A faint crystalline residue that settles on surfaces near mist-type spirit entities. It has a weak cooling property and is collected by medicine disciples for minor qi-soothing applications.

name: Bone Shard
description: A finger-long fragment from a bone hound's frame, still holding a faint animate qi. Useful in formation work or traded to the sect research hall for a modest contribution credit.

name: Bone Dust
description: Fine powder scraped from a bone hound's outer frame. Alchemy uses for it are limited, but blacksmiths occasionally request it for case-hardening mortal-grade metals.

name: Corrupted Qi Bead
description: A dense bead of condensed qi with dark threading through its centre. The corruption is stable but present. Any elder who sees it will have questions. It is worth a great deal to the right buyer and a great deal of trouble to the wrong one.

name: Black-Thread Shard
description: A fragment of the formation token carried by a black-thread cultist. The thread pattern etched into it matches nothing in the sect's documented records. Someone spent time designing this.

name: Lightning Method Scroll
description: A sealed cultivation scroll recovered from the Black-Thread Hollow. The qi threading it is sharp and fast, running counter to cloud methods. It contains a complete instruction for a Lightning elemental strike technique.

name: Void Hollow Root
description: A pale root recovered from the Black-Thread Hollow's formation chamber, where it spent years absorbing qi overflow from the Azure Cloud base formation. Medicine elders would classify it as a Foundation Establishment-tier ingredient.

name: Black-Thread Codex
description: A sealed codex recovered from the hollow's leader. It names the black-thread order, details their philosophy of collecting what spills from great formations, and lists three secondary siphon sites embedded near major sect formations across the region.

name: Standard Qi Pill
description: A properly made outer sect grade pill. Cloudless and clean. A mild impurity trace remains — qi pills are honest about what they cost.

name: Road Healing Draught
description: A thick herbal draught sold along trade roads for wounds that cannot wait. Bitter. Works.

name: Spirit-Grade Shortsword
description: A single-edge shortsword with a hairline of spirit metal folded into the spine. It holds an edge longer than mortal iron and responds more readily to qi channeling.

name: Crude Leather Vest
description: Layers of beast hide stitched over a linen backing. It will not stop a blade cleanly, but it will slow one.

name: Clear-Meridian Tonic
description: A clean, cold tonic that scrapes residue out of shallow meridians. It leaves the foundation slightly raw afterward.

name: Foundation-Steadying Decoction
description: An earthy, antler-rich decoction that settles rough qi before a bottleneck attempt. It steadies the foundation but leaves the body tired.

name: Shadow-Viper Antivenom
description: A sharp antivenom made from the thing that would have killed you. It restores health quickly but leaves a trace of medicinal impurity.

name: Road Wound Poultice
description: A practical field dressing of hide, moss, and bitter paste. It closes wounds well enough to keep moving, though the work taxes the body.

name: Mist-Calming Draught
description: A thin draught that calms agitated qi and carries some impurity out with the breath. The cold bites before it helps.

name: Spirit-Hardened Shortsword
description: A road blade hardened with shell and beast blood until it holds qi along the edge. Brutal, plain, and effective.

name: Viper-Scale Blade
description: A shortsword wrapped and balanced with shadow viper scale. It does not hit hardest, but it moves well in close exchanges.

name: Cloud Stag Spear
description: A bamboo staff refitted with a cloud stag antler point. It carries reach, piercing force, and a faint cold-white qi trace.

name: Reinforced Beast-Hide Vest
description: A beast-hide vest reinforced with bone dust and careful stitching. It is ugly, heavy, and much better than bleeding.

name: Crab Shell Pauldron
description: A heavy shoulder guard cut from stone crab shell and strapped over beast hide. It turns glancing blows into bruises instead of wounds.

name: Cloud-Iron Binding Kit
description: Copper, bone dust, and cloud moss worked into flexible binding strips. Not impressive alone, but essential for keeping spirit materials attached to mortal gear.

name: Spirit-Sealed Staff Wrap
description: A reinforced staff bound with cloud-iron strips and beast blood sealant. It favors guard, leverage, and measured counterstrikes.

name: Mountain Road Gossip
description: A handful of crossroads rumours passed over tea: a white-robed cultivator who paid in high-grade stones without looking at the count, a northern sect posting missing-disciple notices, and quiet attention gathering around the old Jade Spine road east.
